q: A lift-and-shift SQL Server app needs SQL Agent jobs and cross-database queries with minimal changes. Recommend:
e: Managed Instance offers near-100% SQL Server compatibility (Agent, cross-DB) as a managed service; SQL Database lacks these instance features.

q: Which purchasing model allows Azure Hybrid Benefit and independent compute/storage scaling for Azure SQL?
e: vCore decouples compute/storage and supports Hybrid Benefit and reserved capacity; DTU bundles everything.

q: A dev database is used a few hours daily; cost must be minimal with auto-pause when idle. Recommend:
e: Serverless auto-scales and auto-pauses — you pay only storage while paused.

q: A SaaS vendor hosts 400 tenant databases with unpredictable, spiky usage. Most cost-efficient:
e: Elastic pools share eDTU/vCores across databases with complementary usage peaks — the classic multi-tenant answer.

q: Requirement: 60 TB OLTP database, rapid scale-out of read replicas, near-instant restores. Which tier?
e: Hyperscale supports 100+ TB, snapshot-based fast backup/restore, and quickly added named replicas.

q: Which SQL tier includes a free-to-use readable secondary replica via ApplicationIntent=ReadOnly?
e: Business Critical's Always On replicas include a built-in readable secondary for read offloading.

q: In Cosmos DB, what is the default and most commonly recommended consistency level?
e: Session guarantees read-your-own-writes within a client session — the latency/consistency sweet spot.

q: Order Cosmos DB consistency levels strongest to weakest:
o: Strong, Bounded Staleness, Session, Consistent Prefix, Eventual
e: Strong → Bounded Staleness → Session → Consistent Prefix → Eventual.

q: A Cosmos container shows one physical partition throttling while others idle. Root cause?
o: Hot partition from low-cardinality/skewed partition key
e: Skewed partition keys concentrate traffic; choose high-cardinality keys aligned to query patterns.

q: Globally distributed app requires writes accepted in every region with RPO≈0. Configure:
e: Multi-region writes give active-active write availability with conflict resolution policies.

q: Cosmos DB workload is spiky and low volume overall; cheapest throughput model?
e: Serverless bills per consumed RU with no minimum — best for low/spiky traffic; autoscale suits variable but sustained loads.

q: Which storage redundancy survives both a zone outage AND a regional disaster?
e: GZRS = zone-redundant locally + geo-replicated to the pair region; RA-GZRS adds secondary reads.

q: Compliance requires blob data be immutable (WORM) for 7 years. Use:
e: Immutable storage time-based retention enforces write-once-read-many; legal holds cover open-ended cases.

q: Logs accessed rarely after 30 days must auto-move to cheaper tiers and delete after 2 years. Use:
e: Lifecycle rules tier (hot→cool→cold→archive) and expire blobs by age/last-access automatically.

q: Retrieval from which blob tier can take hours and requires rehydration?
e: Archive is offline storage; rehydrate (standard/priority) before reading.

q: Big-data analytics needs a hierarchical filesystem with POSIX ACLs on blob storage. Enable:
e: HNS turns Blob storage into ADLS Gen2 — directories, POSIX ACLs, analytics-optimized.

q: Branch offices need local fast access to a central file share with cloud as source of truth. Recommend:
o: Azure File Sync with cloud tiering on local servers
e: File Sync caches hot files on local Windows servers, tiering cold data to Azure Files.

q: Which is the safest SAS type for delegating temporary blob access?
e: User-delegation SAS is signed with Entra credentials, avoiding account keys and enabling identity-based revocation.

q: Session state and hot lookups need sub-millisecond reads shared across app instances. Recommend:
e: Redis is the in-memory cache for session/hot-path data — classic cache-aside companion.

q: Which feature lets downstream services react to every insert/update in a Cosmos container?
e: Change feed streams document changes — powering event sourcing, materialized views, and integrations.

q: RTO vs RPO: a 4-hour RTO and 15-minute RPO means…
o: Restore within 15 min, lose up to 4 h data
Restore within 4 h, lose at most 15 min of data
e: RTO bounds downtime; RPO bounds data loss.

q: Which statement about backup vs replication is correct?
o: Backup protects against corruption/deletion (point-in-time); replication protects against infrastructure loss — you need the right mix of both
e: Replication faithfully copies corruption/ransomware too; only point-in-time backups recover from it.

q: Which vault type backs up Azure VMs and SQL-in-VM workloads?
e: Recovery Services vaults handle VMs, SQL/SAP in VMs, Files, and MARS/MABS; Backup vaults cover blobs/disks/PostgreSQL.

q: Ransomware protection for backups includes which features?
o: Soft delete, immutable vaults, multi-user authorization
e: Soft delete retains deleted backups, immutability blocks tampering, MUA requires a second identity for destructive ops.

q: A 3-tier app needs regional DR with RTO of minutes, RPO of seconds, and ordered multi-tier failover with scripts. Recommend:
o: Azure Site Recovery with recovery plans
e: ASR continuously replicates and orchestrates failover via recovery plans; backup restores are far slower.

q: How do you validate ASR readiness without disrupting production?
o: Test failover into an isolated network
e: Test failovers spin up replicas in isolated networks — run them regularly.

q: Apps must reconnect automatically to the secondary SQL region after failover without connection string changes. Configure:
e: Failover groups provide stable read-write/read-only listener endpoints that follow the failover.

q: Azure SQL point-in-time restore covers up to 35 days. Requirement: restore capability for 7 years. Add:
e: LTR stores weekly/monthly/yearly full backups up to 10 years.

q: Cheapest availability improvement that survives datacenter failure within a region:
e: Zone redundancy is the low-cost first step; multi-region only where RTO/RPO justify it.

q: Which routing setup implements active-passive regional failover for an HTTP app?
o: Front Door priority routing to primary with health-probe failover to secondary
e: Priority routing sends traffic to the healthy highest-priority origin, failing over automatically.

q: Which service injects controlled faults (zone down, CPU pressure) to validate resilience?
e: Chaos Studio runs fault-injection experiments against your workloads.

q: Storage account with RA-GRS: what does customer-initiated account failover do?
o: Promotes secondary region to primary (potential data loss up to Last Sync Time)
e: Failover promotes the async secondary — data after Last Sync Time is lost; plan around it.

q: Which store do KQL queries in Azure Monitor run against?
e: Logs land in Log Analytics; KQL queries, log alerts, and workbooks operate there.

q: You need distributed tracing across APIM → Container Apps → Service Bus consumers. Use:
e: App Insights provides end-to-end transaction tracing and the Application Map across services.

q: Alert when p95 latency KQL query over 15 min exceeds a threshold. Which alert type?
e: Custom KQL aggregations need log alerts; simple platform metrics use faster metric alerts.

q: What executes notifications and automation when any alert fires?
e: Action groups define receivers (email/SMS/webhook/Function/Logic App/ITSM) reused across alert rules.

q: Platform logs must go to a SIEM outside Azure. Which diagnostic-settings destination fits?
e: Event Hubs streams logs to external SIEM/analytics; Storage is archive; Log Analytics is Azure-native analysis.

q: Teams must query only their own resources' logs in a shared central workspace. Use:
e: Resource-context access scopes queries to resources the user can read — keeping central design with per-team isolation.

q: Which tool proactively probes your public endpoint from multiple regions and alerts on failures?
e: Availability (web) tests run synthetic checks globally with alerting.

q: Azure region incident notifications (platform-side) come from…
e: Service Health covers Azure incidents/maintenance; Resource Health reports your specific resource's state.

q: A SOC needs cross-source security correlation, hunting, and automated response playbooks. Recommend:
e: Sentinel is the cloud-native SIEM/SOAR; Defender for Cloud feeds it posture and workload alerts.

q: Which practices control Log Analytics cost?
o: Retention tuning, archive/long-term tiers, daily caps, Basic table plans, App Insights sampling
e: Cost levers: retention, table plans, caps, and sampling — an exam-favorite operational question.

q: NSG/VNet flow logs and packet capture for network troubleshooting come from…
e: Network Watcher provides flow logs, connection monitor, IP flow verify, and packet capture.
